free_text: Investigation of activation cross sections for deuteron
induced reactions on strontium up to 50 MeV
Sedimented Sr(NO3)2 onto 50um Al backing covered with
10 um Al foil.
The stack was irradiated in a short Faraday cup for
40 min with a 50 MeV, 50 nA deuteron beam.
The thicknesses of the targets were determined by
weight and area measurement, in the case of sedimented
samples the stoichiometry was also taken into account.
Four series of gamma-ray spectra were measured to
follow the decay, started at different times after end
of bombardment (EOB): 3.9-7.1 h, 25.7-45.6 h,
92.1-172.4 h and 1846.2-1282.6 h, respectively.
Nuclear data were taken from NUDAT2.6 online database.
The uncertainty on the median energy in the
foils includes the cumulative effects of uncertainty of
incident beam energy, uncertainty of stopping power of
each foil due to thickness variation and  straggling
effects.
The uncertainty on the cross section values
are estimated in the  standard way following the
recommendations of the ISO-guide as the square root of
the quadratic summation of the independently
contributing parameters. Uncertainty of time parameters
is not included.
surface density of target nuclei(1%),
incident charged particle flux (7%),
detector efficiency (5%)
beam loss correction(1.5%)
statistical and peak fitting (1-20%)
The experimental data were compared to the
results of the theoretical model code TALYS.
The measured experimental data were compared to
those found in the literature.
Fitted excitation functions were used for
determination of integral physical yields.
